diagrams.title: Basic Instruction Word Format
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a generic instruction word showing the division of bits into Operation Code (Op Code) and Address/Operand fields. This helps students understand how instructions are structured in memory.
elements: Op Code Field (highlighted in distinct color)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental structure of a machine instruction, showing how the binary instruction is divided into functional parts that the CPU interprets.

diagrams.title: Types of Instruction Formats
diagrams.description: A comparative diagram showing Zero-Address, One-Address, Two-Address, and Three-Address instruction formats side by side, illustrating how many operand fields each format contains.
elements: Op Code field in each format
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the different ways instructions can be formatted based on the number of operands they contain, helping students understand trade-offs in instruction set design.

diagrams.title: Addressing Modes
diagrams.description: A diagram illustrating various addressing modes including Immediate, Direct, Indirect, Register, Register Indirect, and Indexed addressing, with visual arrows showing how each mode locates the operand.
elements: Direct Addressing (address in instruction points to memory)
Indirect Addressing (address points to pointer in memory)
Register Indirect Addressing (register points to memory)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the different methods by which instructions specify the location of their operands, a fundamental concept in instruction execution and programming.
